us-politics.headline: US Warplanes Shot Down Over Iran, Crew Member Missing
us-politics.body:
Two US warplanes were shot down during operations over Iran, with recovery helicopters also taking fire. One crew member remains missing. The incidents mark a sharp escalation in what has become an active US military campaign against Iranian targets.

The operation is drawing bipartisan unease at home. Republican Senator John Curtis called for ending offensive operations without a formal declaration of war, and Rep. Nancy Mace warned against further escalation and opposed deploying ground troops. A Democrat slammed prediction market Polymarket for taking bets on the missing pilot's fate.

NPR notes Trump's wartime rhetoric differs markedly from past presidents in tone and framing - a distinction analysts say carries real consequences for how allies and adversaries read US intentions.

sources.title: 2 US helicopters hit during recovery efforts in Iran
GOP senator calls to end Iran operations without formal declaration of war
Two U.S. warplanes shot down; search ongoing in Iran for 1 missing crew member
Mace ‘concerned about escalation’ in Iran war
Democrat rips Polymarket for taking bets on fate of US pilot shot down over Iran
How Trump's wartime rhetoric differs from past presidents and why that matters

us-politics.headline: Trump Budget Draws Fire From Both Parties
us-politics.body:
Trump's proposed budget cuts TSA funding, mandates privatisation at small airports, slashes domestic programs, and redirects billions toward defense and a $10 billion DC construction fund. It also seeks $152 million to reopen Alcatraz as a federal prison.

Republican Senator Susan Collins publicly knocked the domestic funding cuts, a rare break from a senior GOP figure. Senate Democrats separately pressed the Treasury Department over plans to add Trump's name to US currency, calling the move inappropriate.

The defense side of the budget expands military spending significantly, with FBI Director Kash Patel and Army Secretary Pete McCormick arguing that terror threats inside the US grew under the Biden administration and require a stronger response.

sources.title: Susan Collins knocks funding cuts in White House budget proposal
What to know about Trump’s defense budget proposal
Senate Democrats press Treasury Department on decision to add Trump name to currency
Trump proposes $10B fund for D.C. construction, beautification projects
Trump budget would cut TSA funding, require privatization at small airports
Trump seeks $152M to rebuild Alcatraz as ‘state-of-the-art secure prison’
Patel, McCormick warn foreign terror threats inside US grew during Biden years

us-politics.headline: DOJ Reshuffles as Blanche Takes Top Job
us-politics.body:
Todd Blanche, Trump's former personal attorney, has taken over as the acting head of the Justice Department following Pam Bondi's departure. Blanche told reporters there is no evidence in the Epstein files that Epstein worked as a spy. Rep. Mace said she is not backing away from her push to have Bondi testify about those files.

Separately, Senator Thom Tillis set a clear condition for any incoming attorney general nominee - no moves against those convicted over January 6. Trump is also weighing broader Cabinet changes but wants to avoid what aides describe as a massive shake-up, with figures including Howard Lutnick and Todd Blanche among those being assessed for expanded roles.

sources.title: Todd Blanche takes on DOJ’s top job and the burden of Trump’s expectations
Blanche: No evidence in Epstein files he was a spy
Tillis sets Jan. 6 red line on attorney general nominee
Mace ‘not backing away’ from Bondi testifying on Epstein
Trump mulls more Cabinet changes — but wants to avoid ‘massive shake-up’

us-politics.headline: Legal Battles Flare Over ICE, Voting, and Colorado Courts
us-politics.body:
Democratic-led states filed suit against Trump's executive order restricting mail-in ballots, arguing it oversteps federal authority over state election administration. The White House has not yet responded publicly to the litigation.

In Colorado, lawyers report that the state court e-filing system now requires them to certify they will not assist ICE - a requirement critics say puts attorneys in an impossible position between state and federal obligations. Illinois Governor Pritzker called on Trump officials to testify about the ICE crackdown in his state. The White House dismissed the move as a political stunt.

Vice President Vance was tapped as a so-called fraud czar, with Trump directing him to investigate what the administration characterises as taxpayer theft in Democratic-governed states.

sources.title: Colorado lawyers say court e-file system now makes them certify they won’t assist ICE
Democratic-led states sue over Trump mail-in ballot order
Vance tapped as ‘fraud czar’ as Trump targets blue states over taxpayer theft
Pritzker calls on Trump officials to testify over ICE crackdown, White House blasts move as ‘political stunt’

au-politics.headline: Tax policy divides parties as election pressure mounts
au-politics.body:
Both Labor and the Coalition are scrambling to match voter expectations on tax ahead of the federal election, with the Guardian reporting that even Liberal defence spokesman Andrew Hastie has suggested miners could pay more. The debate signals fractures within the Coalition over resources taxation, while Albanese faces pressure to offer meaningful relief without blowing the budget.

The Spectator's conservative commentary flags broader concerns about policy drift and what critics see as missed opportunities in the political debate. With cost-of-living still the dominant voter concern, neither major party has landed a clear, credible position. The gap between rhetoric and policy detail is widening.

sources.title: Taxing times for Albanese and Taylor as parties seek to match voter expectations
What did I miss?

au-politics.headline: One Nation leaves SA Liberals fighting for opposition status
au-politics.body:
South Australia's Liberal Party is clinging to its position as the official opposition after One Nation's strong showing left the party's numbers in doubt. The situation has few modern precedents at state level, raising questions about parliamentary procedure and whether the Liberals can maintain the resources and speaking rights that come with opposition status.

The result reflects a broader pattern of minor-party and independent gains eroding the two-party system in Australian state politics. For the Liberals, it compounds a difficult period of rebuilding after successive electoral setbacks. One Nation's rise in SA adds another variable to an already complicated federal election landscape.

sources.title: 'So few precedents': One Nation leaves SA Liberals clinging to opposition status

au-politics.headline: Sunken trawler unravels alleged Victorian cocaine syndicate
au-politics.body:
Victorian police have made arrests after a sunken fishing trawler led investigators to an alleged large-scale cocaine smuggling operation. The vessel's discovery triggered a chain of evidence that exposed the alleged syndicate's supply network, according to ABC reporting.

The case highlights the use of commercial fishing boats as cover for drug importation, a method authorities have flagged as a growing concern. Details of the arrests and charges are still emerging.

sources.title: Sunken trawler alerts police to alleged drug-smuggling plan

au-politics.headline: Iran shoots down US jet, raising stakes for Trump
au-politics.body:
Iran has downed a US aircraft, a development ABC analysis describes as likely to shock Americans on multiple fronts - military, diplomatic and political. The incident lands at a volatile moment, with the Trump administration already managing tensions across the Middle East and facing questions about its strategic posture toward Tehran.

ABC's analysis warns the episode could become a defining test for Trump, drawing comparisons to past presidential quagmires triggered by sudden military escalation. How Washington responds in the coming days will be closely watched by allies in the Pacific, including Australia.

sources.title: Iran's downing of a US jet will shock Americans in three ways

world.headline: US-Iran War Escalates as Hormuz Shipping Slowly Resumes
world.body:
Iran shot down two US warplanes and closed the Strait of Hormuz as fighting intensified, marking a sharp escalation in the conflict. The WHO reports more than 20 attacks on Iranian healthcare facilities since March 1, while survivors of a US-Israeli bridge strike described scenes of mass panic and destruction.

The strait showed signs of cautious reopening, with Omani, French, and Japanese vessels completing transits - the first commercial shipping to pass through since the war began. Whether that holds remains uncertain. Israel simultaneously struck bridges in Lebanon, where Christians observed Good Friday under active bombardment. A separate attack on the UAE's Habshan gas facility killed at least one person before being intercepted.

sources.title: Iran war live: Tehran downs 2 US warplanes; Israel bombs Lebanon bridges
Will force be used to reopen Strait of Hormuz?
Survivors of Iran bridge attack recall terror of US-Israeli strike
More than 20 attacks on Iranian healthcare facilities since March 1: WHO
At least one killed at UAE’s Habshan gas facility after intercepted attack
French-owned ship passes through Strait of Hormuz
What we know about the US fighter jet shot down in Iran
Omani, French and Japanese vessels transit the Strait of Hormuz
Drone footage shows a factory in Israel damaged by Iranian missile debris

world.headline: Russia Drones Ukraine; Sudan Hospital Strike Kills Ten
world.body:
Footage released Friday shows a Russian drone striking a residential apartment block in Ukraine, the latest in a sustained campaign targeting civilian infrastructure. The attack drew renewed international condemnation but no new commitments of military support.

In Sudan, a drone strike killed ten people at a hospital, according to medical charity MSF. The strike follows a pattern of attacks on protected sites that has drawn repeated warnings from humanitarian organisations. Two conflicts, two drone strikes on civilians - within the same 24-hour news cycle.

sources.title: Video shows the moment a Russian drone hit an apartment in Ukraine
Drone strike on Sudan hospital kills 10, medical charity MSF says

world.headline: Cuba Frees 2,000 Prisoners; US States Sue Over Ballots
world.body:
Cuba began releasing more than 2,000 prisoners as US pressure intensified, a significant concession from Havana that follows months of diplomatic and economic leverage applied by Washington. The releases are ongoing and the full scope of conditions attached remains unclear.

Inside the US, two dozen Democrat-led states filed suit against the Trump administration over restrictions on mail-in voting, opening a broad legal front ahead of future elections. Separately, a federal judge upheld the dismissal of subpoenas targeting Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, blocking what critics called an attempt to pressure the central bank.

sources.title: Cuba begins releasing more than 2,000 prisoners as US pressure mounts
Cuba releases over 2,000 prisoners amid mounting US pressure
Two dozen Democrat-led states sue Trump over mail-in ballot limits
US judge upholds decision to toss subpoenas into Fed Chair Jerome Powell

world.headline: Artemis II Crew Sends Back Stunning Earth Images
world.body:
NASA's Artemis II crew captured and released striking photographs of Earth from deep space, offering the most vivid imagery of the planet from a crewed mission in decades. The images were taken as the spacecraft continues its lunar flyby mission - the first crewed trip to the Moon's vicinity since Apollo.

The photos drew wide attention at a moment when much of the world's focus remains fixed on conflict. NASA described the images as among the most detailed ever taken of Earth from a crewed vehicle.

sources.title: Artemis II crew take 'spectacular' image of Earth
NASA releases stunning first photos of Earth from Artemis II moon mission

ai.headline: Anthropic Blocks OpenClaw, Enters Biotech, Launches PAC
ai.body:
Anthropic had a busy week. The company effectively cut off OpenClaw - a third-party Claude client - by requiring its users to pay extra beyond their existing subscriptions, drawing complaints on Hacker News from affected Claude Code subscribers. Separately, Ars Technica flagged fresh security concerns around OpenClaw, advising users to assume their accounts may be compromised.

Beyond the platform drama, Anthropic reportedly acquired biotech startup Coefficient Bio for $400 million, signaling a push into life sciences. The company also launched a political action committee, stepping up its Washington presence as AI regulation debates intensify.

sources.title: Anthropic essentially bans OpenClaw from Claude by making subscribers pay extra
Extra usage credit for Claude to celebrate usage bundles launch (Pro, Max, Team)
Tell HN: Anthropic no longer allowing Claude Code subscriptions to use OpenClaw
OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security

Anthropic ramps up its political activities with a new PAC

ai.headline: OpenAI Reshuffles Executives as Key Leaders Step Back
ai.body:
OpenAI is reorganizing at the top. COO Brad Lightcap is moving into a new "special projects" role, while both Fidji Simo and the company's AGI lead are taking leaves of absence. The timing is awkward - OpenAI is navigating its ongoing restructuring from nonprofit to for-profit while managing intense competitive pressure.

The departures leave visible gaps in leadership across operations and long-term AI development. No replacements have been announced.

sources.title: OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead ‘special projects’
OpenAI’s AGI boss is taking a leave of absence
OpenAI’s Fidji Simo Is Taking Medical Leave Amid an Executive Shake-Up

ai.headline: AI Is Eroding Critical Thinking, New Research Warns
ai.body:
A new study finds that heavy AI users are increasingly outsourcing their reasoning to language models, a pattern researchers call "cognitive surrender." Participants showed reduced willingness to question or verify AI outputs, even when those outputs were wrong. The effect was stronger among frequent users.

The findings land as AI tools embed deeper into workplaces and schools. Critics argue the industry has little incentive to design tools that push back on users or encourage independent thought.

sources.title: "Cognitive surrender" leads AI users to abandon logical thinking, research finds

ai.headline: Musk Ties SpaceX IPO Banks to Grok Subscriptions
ai.body:
Elon Musk is reportedly requiring investment banks competing for SpaceX's IPO mandate to purchase Grok subscriptions, effectively using a multi-billion-dollar deal as leverage to drive revenue to his AI venture xAI. The move blurs the line between business negotiation and coercion.

Meanwhile, Trump's broader AI data center push is stalling - undercut by his own tariffs raising hardware costs - and a Meta data breach via contractor Mercor exposed sensitive AI industry information. The week illustrated how political and corporate power are increasingly tangled with AI infrastructure.

sources.title: Elon Musk insists banks working on SpaceX IPO must buy Grok subscriptions
Trump ignores biggest reasons his AI data center buildout is failing
Meta Pauses Work With Mercor After Data Breach Puts AI Industry Secrets at Risk

btc.headline: Circle Faces Heat Over $285M Hack and Compliance Failures
btc.body:
Circle is under serious pressure after the $285 million Drift protocol hack exposed the stablecoin issuer's reluctance to freeze stolen USDC. Critics argue Circle's inaction undermines one of the core arguments for regulated stablecoins - that issuers can act as a backstop against theft.

On-chain investigator ZachXBT piled on, accusing Circle of $420 million in compliance failures since 2022. The back-to-back allegations paint a damaging picture of a company preparing for a public listing while struggling to police its own network.

The controversy arrives at a critical moment. With stablecoin legislation moving through Congress, Circle's critics now have concrete examples to argue that USDC's compliance record does not match its regulatory-friendly branding.

sources.title: Circle under fire after $285 million Drift hack over inaction to freeze stolen USDC
ZachXBT accuses Circle of $420M in 'compliance failures' since 2022

btc.headline: Schwab Eyes Spot Crypto Trading as Bitcoin Dips Below $70K
btc.body:
Charles Schwab confirmed plans to launch direct spot trading for Bitcoin and Ether in the first half of 2026, a move that would bring one of America's largest retail brokerages fully into the crypto market. The announcement signals that institutional appetite for crypto infrastructure remains intact despite recent price weakness.

Bitcoin slipped below $70,000 this week, putting pressure on corporate treasury strategies. Some firms that followed MicroStrategy's playbook are now reassessing their positions, creating a visible split between committed Bitcoin holders and those treating it as a tactical trade.

One former hedge fund manager warned that Bitcoin may be underpricing the risk of a prolonged Iran conflict, adding a geopolitical dimension to an already uncertain macro backdrop. Strong U.S. jobs data - 178,000 added in March - gave broader markets a lift but offered little immediate relief for crypto.

sources.title: Schwab plans spot bitcoin, ether trading launch in first half of 2026
Is Bitcoin mispricing a prolonged Iran war? Ex-hedge fund manager weighs in
Crypto Biz: Bitcoin treasuries break ranks as BTC dips below $70K
U.S. March jobs smash expectations, with 178,000 added

btc.headline: Coinbase Trust Charter Draws Community Bank Opposition
btc.body:
A coalition of U.S. community banks is pushing back against the OCC's move to grant Coinbase a federal trust charter, arguing it would give the crypto exchange access to banking privileges without equivalent regulatory burdens. The opposition reflects growing anxiety among traditional lenders as crypto firms pursue direct access to the financial system.

The fight over Coinbase's charter is part of a broader regulatory tug-of-war. In Europe, Malta is clashing with ESMA over crypto oversight, with smaller jurisdictions resisting centralized EU control. Cambodia, meanwhile, is taking a harder line domestically - lawmakers there proposed severe prison sentences for crypto scammers this week.

Taken together, the moves show regulators worldwide tightening their grip, even as they disagree sharply on who should hold the reins.

sources.title: US community banks oppose OCC's approval of Coinbase trust charter
Centralizing crypto: Why Malta’s clash with ESMA is about more than one small state
Cambodian lawmakers propose severe prison time for crypto scammers

btc.headline: Ethereum Staking Milestone Nears as L2 Scaling Debate Heats Up
btc.body:
The Ethereum Foundation is close to hitting its 70,000 staked ETH target, a milestone that reinforces the network's shift toward a staking-driven security model. The progress comes as Offchain Labs argued that Ethereum Layer 2 networks need more responsive fee pricing to handle billions of users at scale.

The two developments are connected. Staking growth strengthens Ethereum's base layer, but L2s remain the practical path to mass adoption - and their fee mechanisms are still catching up. Hyperliquid's entry into the top 10 crypto derivatives platforms in Q1, per CoinGlass data, shows that newer execution layers are already pulling volume away from established venues.

Meanwhile, decentralized email platform Dmail announced it will shut down on May 15, a reminder that not every Web3 infrastructure bet survives long enough to find its users.

sources.title: Ethereum Foundation nearly reaches 70,000 staked ETH goal
Ethereum L2s need responsive pricing to scale, says Offchain Labs
Binance led Q1 crypto derivatives as Hyperliquid cracked top 10: CoinGlass
Decentralized email platform Dmail to cease services on May 15

food.name: Miso-Glazed Black Cod
food.description: Silky, buttery cod fillets marinated in white miso, mirin, and sake then broiled until caramelised and lacquered
food.tip: Marinate for at least 24 hours — the longer the miso sits on the fish, the deeper the umami crust you get under the broiler

exercise.name: The Deck of Cards Workout
exercise.equipment: A standard deck of playing cards, some floor space
exercise.description: Assign a bodyweight move to each suit — push-ups, squats, lunges, burpees — then flip cards one by one and perform the reps shown, making every session completely unpredictable and surprisingly brutal

learn.topic: The Ship of Theseus and Personal Identity
learn.description: The ancient paradox asks whether an object that has had all its components replaced remains fundamentally the same object — and philosophers from Locke to Parfit have weaponised it to ask the same unsettling question about you, since every cell in your body is eventually replaced
learn.why: On a quiet Saturday it's a perfect rabbit hole because it connects ancient Greek thought to modern neuroscience and questions of consciousness, and it will genuinely make you uncertain about something you assumed was obvious — namely, whether the person who wakes up tomorrow is really you
